title: Translation Units, Declarations, Diagnostics, and Linking · Memory Trace: Diagnostic Baseline
summary: A portable telemetry library compiles in one file but fails or silently changes behavior after its API is split across files. Planned artifact: Add and verify the cmem diagnostic baseline increment in warning-clean multi-file interface dossier. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Compile with strict language mode, high-value warnings, warnings as errors, debug information, and a documented extension policy.
Use complete prototypes and compatible parameter and return types across every caller boundary.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Predict Diagnostic Baseline · Theory Diagnostic Baseline
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory native-systems team handles memory case 2870. Produce bounded C evidence to model translation and diagnostic gates before execution; change the size, lifetime, failure, or ownership condition; print the observed result and invariant. Instant code stays inside the PicoC practice subset. C23 syntax, current GCC and Clang, optimization, ABI, threads, sanitizers, analyzers, fuzzers, operating-system resources, foreign runtimes, load, and production behavior require named controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Compile with strict language mode, high-value warnings, warnings as errors, debug information, and a documented extension policy.

steps.title: Build Diagnostic Baseline evidence · Theory Diagnostic Baseline
steps.instruction:
For this theory demonstration of cmem-diagnostic-baseline, write the complete compile-ready C program headed "/* Evidence: cmem-diagnostic-baseline-ngu223 */" for changed case 8906. It must model translation and diagnostic gates before execution, reject one changed boundary, print an observable result and invariant, and return nonzero when the invariant fails. Keep instant code within the PicoC 3.2.2 practice subset; do not claim C23, optimizing compiler, sanitizer, ABI, thread, operating-system, FFI, load, or production evidence until its explicit controlled transfer gate passes.
